jokes.text: Tony tells story about foreign passenger asking if $100 is enough for a $17 fare, then reveals he gave $83 change instead of keeping the obvious tip
jokes.setup: Tony builds up story about lucky cabbie getting huge tip from confused foreign passenger
jokes.punchline: Tony's honest nature leads him to give proper change, missing obvious windfall
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Tony's naive honesty. The setup builds expectation of windfall, then subverts with his earnest morality. Strong structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Louie's physical reaction to Tony's story - nearly fainting at the missed opportunity
jokes.setup: Tony's revelation about giving back the money
jokes.punchline: Louie's visceral shock at the financial stupidity
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that contrasts Louie's mercenary nature with Tony's honesty. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: You don't have to thank me... It'd be fun.
jokes.setup: Alex just agreed to watch a kid without listening
jokes.punchline: He acts confident about something he knows nothing about
jokes.explanation: Mild irony - Alex's false confidence about childcare sets up his later panic.

jokes.text: Sure, I'll take your date out. What's she look like?
jokes.setup: Alex asks Bobby to help with babysitting
jokes.punchline: Bobby misunderstands and offers to steal Alex's date instead
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that reveals Bobby's self-centered opportunism in a funny way.

jokes.text: John wanting to be asked to help even though he can't, just for the feeling of being included
jokes.setup: Everyone else is being asked to help with Jason
jokes.punchline: John admits he just wanted to be asked despite being unavailable
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing John's need for inclusion. Well-integrated with his personality and creates gentle cringe humor.

jokes.text: Aah, Alex, I don't know anything about taking care of kids.
jokes.setup: Bobby just offered to help with childcare
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's completely unqualified
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction - Bobby offers help then immediately disqualifies himself.

jokes.text: Bobby's awkward conversation opener: 'So you're a kid, huh?'
jokes.setup: Bobby left alone with Jason and needs to make conversation
jokes.punchline: States the obvious in most awkward way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of adult awkwardness with children. Economy of language creates maximum cringe effect.

jokes.text: Tony suggests Alex 'do what you do when you were a kid' and Alex responds 'You want me to punch him?'
jokes.setup: Tony gives advice about relating to the kid
jokes.punchline: Alex reveals his childhood involved violence
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal that explains Alex's discomfort with children. Sharp timing and economy.

jokes.text: Louie's phone conversation: 'And how fat are you?' to a customer complaint
jokes.setup: Louie taking a customer complaint about rudeness
jokes.punchline: Louie being even ruder while pretending to help
jokes.explanation: Pure Louie - using customer service as excuse for more cruelty. Perfect character integration with economical delivery.

jokes.text: Tony's comment about Jason beating his brains out after two boxing lessons, followed by 'What took him so long?' from someone
jokes.setup: Tony praising Jason's boxing progress
jokes.punchline: Implied that beating Tony's brains out shouldn't take long
jokes.explanation: Standard insult humor playing on Tony's reputation for being punching bag. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Louie finishing phone call: 'especially you blimpos' while trying to sound professional
jokes.setup: Louie giving professional-sounding customer service speech
jokes.punchline: Ends with insulting the customer he's supposedly helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier phone conversation, showing Louie can't maintain professionalism even when trying. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Louie's self-congratulatory speech about handling 'delicate situations with tact' after insulting a customer
jokes.setup: Louie just finished being incredibly rude to customer
jokes.punchline: Claims he handles things with tact and delicacy
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic self-awareness. Louie's complete lack of self-knowledge creates perfect dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Louie's bonding moment with Jason: 'I don't dislike you as much as I do most kids'
jokes.setup: Louie attempting to be nice to Jason
jokes.punchline: His version of a compliment is incredibly backhanded
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - his attempt at warmth is still insulting. Shows character growth while maintaining his essential nature.

jokes.text: Louie's parenting advice to Jason about smoking, drinking, and foul language: 'Yeah, it's really tough without a father to teach you'
jokes.setup: Louie asking if Jason has picked up typical vices
jokes.punchline: Implies fathers are supposed to teach kids bad habits
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about masculinity and bad parenting. Subverts expectation of what fathers should teach sons.

jokes.text: John's movie review: 'I sat through it three times. It was that good, huh? Nah, I just couldn't remember where I came in.'
jokes.setup: John appearing to give high praise for movie he saw three times
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was confusion, not enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke. Sets up expectation of enthusiasm then deflates with honest confusion. Good structure.

jokes.text: John offering to spoil the movie ending: 'Or was that the middle?'
jokes.setup: John offering to tell how movie ends
jokes.punchline: Admits he's still confused about the plot
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces John's confusion about the movie. Economical but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: The extended awkward exchange where everyone looks around avoiding suggesting Louie babysit
jokes.setup: They need someone to babysit Jason
jokes.punchline: Everyone pretends not to see obvious solution (Louie)
jokes.explanation: Good sustained awkward comedy but goes on too long. The joke is clear early but gets stretched thin.

jokes.text: Alex finally suggesting Louie followed by 'Nah, I don't want Jason to catch his cold'
jokes.setup: Finally suggesting the obvious choice of Louie
jokes.punchline: Rejecting him for ridiculous reason (his personality being contagious)
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the awkward avoidance. Treating Louie's nastiness as literally contagious is clever metaphor.

jokes.text: Louie smoking his mother's leftover cigar: 'Ma couldn't finish it, so...'
jokes.setup: Alex asking about Louie smoking cigars
jokes.punchline: Reveals his elderly mother was smoking it first
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Louie's relationship with his tough mother. Visual of tiny Louie finishing his mother's cigar is funny.

jokes.text: Louie's mother spilling beer on her dress and being too self-conscious to come inside
jokes.setup: Explaining why his mother won't come inside
jokes.punchline: She's embarrassed about spilled beer, implying it's uncharacteristic
jokes.explanation: Adds to the characterization of Louie's tough mother as someone who drinks beer but has standards. Mild humor.

jokes.text: Car horn honking followed by Louie yelling 'Keep your pants on, crazy lady!' to his mother
jokes.setup: Mother honking impatiently in car
jokes.punchline: Louie's disrespectful response to his own mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - even his mother gets no respect. Shows consistency of character across relationships.

jokes.text: Uh, that's my daughter. Hmm... Well, she's a loser.
jokes.setup: Father proudly identifies his daughter as competitor
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately insults the child to her father's face
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - no social awareness, insulting other people's children directly to their parents.

jokes.text: Louie trying to bet on the spelling bee: '20 bucks says my kid beats your kid' to the other parent
jokes.setup: Formal spelling bee with proper parents in attendance
jokes.punchline: Louie treating it like a sporting event to gamble on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's inability to read social situations. Creates appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: Parent's response 'I do not bet on my child' and Louie's comeback 'That's 'cause you know she's a loser'
jokes.setup: Parent giving dignified refusal to gambling
jokes.punchline: Louie turning it into insult about their child
jokes.explanation: Escalates the inappropriate behavior perfectly. Louie's logic is twisted but consistent with his character.

jokes.text: Tony's boxing commentary: 'The first round is always the toughest. You ought to know, Banta. It's the only one you ever fight in.'
jokes.setup: Tony giving boxing wisdom about first rounds
jokes.punchline: Someone pointing out Tony never lasts past first round
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff using Tony's boxing reputation. Clean structure and good timing.

jokes.text: You ought to know, Banta. It's the only one you ever fight in.
jokes.setup: Tony says first round is always the toughest
jokes.punchline: Louie implies Tony always loses in the first round
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insult - takes Tony's innocent observation and turns it into personal attack.

jokes.text: Guys cheering 'Jason! Jason! Jason!' and explaining 'We're his father' when asked to quiet down
jokes.setup: Loud cheering disrupting formal spelling bee
jokes.punchline: Claiming collective fatherhood to justify behavior
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdist moment showing their collective adoption of Jason. Good emotional core with humor.

jokes.text: Lucky guess.
jokes.setup: Christa correctly spells 'onomatopoeia'
jokes.punchline: Louie refuses to give her credit for obvious skill
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie - can't admit when he's wrong about the competition.

jokes.text: Louie admitting 'I know... I hate her' when told to give Christa credit
jokes.setup: Being told the competing child is good
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of irrational hatred for child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - acknowledging truth while maintaining pettiness. Dark but honest character consistency.

jokes.text: Louie complaining 'Why does she get all the easy ones?' about a very difficult word
jokes.setup: Child gets extremely difficult word 'sesquipedalian'
jokes.punchline: Louie calling it 'easy' out of bias
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor about competition bias. Louie's warped perspective on obvious difficulty is funny.

jokes.text: Fix! Fix!
jokes.setup: Jason loses the spelling bee
jokes.punchline: Louie accuses the competition of being rigged
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie response - can't accept fair defeat, must be conspiracy. Shows his protective investment in Jason.

jokes.text: Tony's consolation: 'Coming in second ain't the worst thing in the world. Right. Tony does it all the time.'
jokes.setup: Tony trying to console Jason about losing
jokes.punchline: Someone using Tony's own record of failure as example
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor using Tony's established pattern of losing. Clean structure and timing.

jokes.text: Louie's response to Jason's mature self-blame: 'If I had to choose between your winning or losing and saying what you just said... I'd still take winning, but this was close.'
jokes.setup: Jason gives mature, self-reflective response about losing
jokes.punchline: Louie admits he'd prefer winning but grudgingly acknowledges character growth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Louie's honesty and gradual softening. Perfect balance of cynicism and warmth. Highly quotable structure.

jokes.text: If I had to choose between your winning or losing and saying what you just said... I'd still take winning, but this was close.
jokes.setup: Jason shows maturity by taking responsibility for his loss
jokes.punchline: Louie pretends to value character over winning, then admits he'd prefer winning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up expectation of Louie showing growth, then undercuts it perfectly while still showing affection for Jason.

jokes.text: Louie sharing his 12-year-old Scotch and someone saying 'Whenever Louie shares anything, it's an occasion'
jokes.setup: Louie offering to share expensive scotch
jokes.punchline: Recognition that Louie sharing anything is unprecedented
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Louie's usual selfishness. Sets up his emotional growth moment.

jokes.text: Bobby's explanation about waiting to have kids: 'we decided to wait until we find out how... I mean, how to raise them'
jokes.setup: Bobby talking about waiting to have children
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implies he doesn't know the mechanics, then corrects to parenting skills
jokes.explanation: Typical Bobby accidental innuendo followed by innocent clarification. Good character moment but predictable.

jokes.text: Louie's lament about finding a woman: 'First you got to find a woman... You got to fall in love... get married... talk to her. Hmm. It ain't worth it.'
jokes.setup: Louie listing requirements for having a family
jokes.punchline: The final requirement 'talk to her' being the dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure where communication becomes the impossible hurdle. Perfect character integration showing Louie's isolation.

jokes.text: Alex's long speech about parenting worries ending with 'All in all... it's not a bad deal'
jokes.setup: Extensive litany of parenting anxieties and sacrifices
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling it 'not a bad deal'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with perfect understated payoff. Shows Alex's wisdom and warmth through humor.

jokes.text: The entire 'Sonny Boy' duet between Louie and his mother with call-and-response commentary
jokes.setup: Touching mother-son bonding moment through song
jokes.punchline: Louie's sarcastic commentary during sentimental song creates absurd contrast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist ending that shows Louie's relationship with his mother. Balances sentiment with cynical humor perfectly. Goes on a bit long but emotionally effective.
